title: gilia gracilis subvar. congesta
intro: Gilia gracilis subvar. congesta, also known as congested gilia, is a variety of the Gilia gracilis plant, a flowering perennial native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils. The health benefits of Gilia gracilis subvar. congesta include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues. Gilia congesta can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for various herbal preparations.
